title: refactor(gen): split static data out of docs.ts
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
- Moves `CODE_SAMPLES` + `RESPONSE_EXAMPLES` into `docs-samples.ts`
- Moves the CSS strings (header, Scalar overrides, guides) into `docs-styles.ts`
- `docs.ts` drops 1596 → 821 lines — pure move, no behavior change
- Logic, schema merge, markdown assembly, and routes stay in `docs.ts`
- Verified: `docs.test.ts` (6/6), `tsc`, biome all green

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

gist.headline: Spring cleaning the docs
gist.blurb: We've reorganized the documentation codebase, splitting out static samples and styles to keep things tidy and maintainable.
gist.summary: Extracted static code samples, response examples, and CSS strings from docs.ts into dedicated docs-samples.ts and docs-styles.ts files.
gist.impact: Developers working on the API gateway will find a much leaner docs.ts file (reduced from 1596 to 821 lines) with no changes to end-user documentation behavior.
gist.visual_concept: The bee mascot and the monitor robot organizing files or cleaning up a workspace, representing code refactoring and organization.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot and a chunky monitor-head robot organizing glowing digital folders in a tidy wooden shed. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
